text: Strength of gravitational force compared to strong nuclear force
explanation: The gravitational force between two bodies is \(F_G = 10^{-49}\) N. The strong nuclear force between two nucleons is \(F_{nuclear} = 10^{-10}\) N. Therefore, the ratio is \(\frac{F_G}{F_{nuclear}} = \frac{10^{-49}}{10^{-10}} = 10^{-39}\).
name: Questions Item 1

text: Dimensions of \(\varepsilon_0\) are
explanation: From Coulomb's law \(F = \frac{1}{4\pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}\), we get \(\varepsilon_0 = \frac{q_1 q_2}{4\pi F r^2}\). Substituting dimensions: \([q] = [AT]\), \([F] = [MLT^{-2}]\), \([r] = [L]\) yields \([\varepsilon_0] = [M^{-1}L^{-3}T^{4}A^{2}]\).
name: Questions Item 2

text: Choose the physical quantity pair with the same dimensions
explanation: Work = \([F][s] = [MLT^{-2}][L] = [ML^{2}T^{-2}]\). Torque = \([F][r] = [MLT^{-2}][L] = [ML^{2}T^{-2}]\). Both have identical dimensions. Angular momentum = \([ML^{2}T^{-1}]\), potential energy = \([ML^{2}T^{-2}]\), linear momentum = \([MLT^{-1}]\), kinetic energy = \([ML^{2}T^{-2}]\), velocity = \([LT^{-1}]\).
name: Questions Item 3

text: To estimate \(g\) from \(g = 4\pi^{2}\frac{L}{T^{2}}\), error in measurement of \(L\) is \(\pm 2\%\) and error in measurement of \(T\) is \(\pm 3\%\). The error in estimated \(g\) will be
explanation: \(\frac{\Delta g}{g} = \frac{\Delta L}{L} + 2\frac{\Delta T}{T}\). Percentage error = \(2\% + 2 \times 3\% = 8\%\). Hence \(\pm 8\%\).
name: Questions Item 4

text: The SI unit of length is ‘metre’. Suppose we adopt a new unit of length which equals \(x\) metre. Then, the area of \(1\,\text{m}^{2}\) expressed in terms of new unit has a magnitude
explanation: New unit length = \(x\) metre, so 1 m = \(\frac{1}{x}\) new units. Area of 1 m² = \(1\,\text{m} \times 1\,\text{m} = \frac{1}{x} \times \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{x^{2}}\) in new units.
name: Questions Item 5

text: The unit of magnetic induction is
explanation: Magnetic flux \(\Phi = B \cdot A\), so \(B = \frac{\Phi}{A}\). Unit of \(\Phi\) is Wb and area is m², hence unit of \(B\) is Wb m^{-2}.
name: Questions Item 6

text: Which of the following statement is correct? (A) Becquerel, who discovered natural radioactivity, belongs to France. (B) Marconi, who discovered wireless telegraphy, was an American. (C) Newton was an American, who discovered the laws of motion. (D) Einstein belongs to England, who simplifies the laws of photoelectric effects.
explanation: Only statement (A) is factually correct. Marconi was Italian, Newton was English, Einstein was German.
name: Questions Item 7

text: Which of the following is not a unit of time?
explanation: Lunar month, leap year and microsecond are units of time. Light year is a unit of distance (distance travelled by light in one year).
name: Questions Item 8

text: Which among the following has dimensions of charge?
explanation: From Gauss’s law \(E \cdot ds = \frac{Q_{enc}}{\varepsilon_0}\), so \(Q_{enc} = \varepsilon_0 E \cdot ds\). Thus dimensions of \(\varepsilon_0 E \cdot ds\) are same as charge.
name: Questions Item 9

text: If the charge of electron \(e\), mass of electron \(m\), speed of light in vacuum \(c\) and Planck’s constant \(h\) are taken as fundamental quantities, then the permeability of vacuum \(\mu_0\) can be expressed as
explanation: Let \(\mu_0 = k e^a m^b c^c h^d\). Equating dimensions: \([M L T^{-2} A^{-2}]\) gives \(a = -2\), \(b = 0\), \(c = -1\), \(d = 1\). Hence \(\mu_0 = \frac{h}{c e^{2}}\).
name: Questions Item 10

text: A physical quantity obtained from the ratio of the coefficient of thermal conductivity to the universal gravitational constant has a dimensional formula \([M^{2a} L^{4b} T^{2c} K^{d}]\), then the value of \(\frac{a+b}{c+b} - d\) is
name: Questions Item 11

text: Two intervals of time are measured as \(\Delta t_1 = (2.00 \pm 0.02)\) s and \(\Delta t_2 = (4.00 \pm 0.02)\) s. The value of \(\sqrt{(\Delta t_1)(\Delta t_2)}\) with correct significant figures and error is
explanation: \(\sqrt{2.00 \times 4.00} = 2.828427\) s. Both values have 3 significant figures, so result = 2.83 s. Relative error = \(\frac{1}{2} (0.02/2.00 + 0.02/4.00) \times 2.8284 \approx 0.021\) s, rounded to 0.01 s. Hence \(2.83 \pm 0.01\) s.
name: Questions Item 12

text: \(N\) divisions on the main scale of a vernier calipers coincide with \((N+1)\) division of the vernier scale. If each division of main scale is \(a\) units, then the least count of the calipers is
explanation: 1 VSD = \(\frac{N}{N+1}\) MSD. Least count = 1 MSD − 1 VSD = \(a - \frac{N}{N+1}a = \frac{a}{N+1}\).
name: Questions Item 13

text: If \(1\%\) and \(2\%\) are the errors in the measurement of mass and density of a cube respectively, then the error in the measurement of length is
explanation: \(d = \frac{m}{l^3}\), so \(l = (m/d)^{1/3}\). \(\frac{\Delta l}{l} = \frac{1}{3} \left( \frac{\Delta m}{m} + \frac{\Delta d}{d} ight)\). Percentage error = \(\frac{1}{3}(1 + 2) = 1\%\).
name: Questions Item 14

text: If \(A\) represents density, \(B\) represents velocity, \(C\) represents specific heat capacity and \(D\) represents wavelength, then the quantity having the dimensions of product of \(A\), \(B\), \(C\) and \(D\) is
name: Questions Item 15

text: Assertion (A) The number 0.00764 has three significant figures. Reason (R) If the number is less than 1, the zeros on the right of the decimal point but to the left of the first non-zero digit are not significant.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
explanation: In 0.00764, leading zeros after decimal are not significant. Only 764 are significant → 3 significant figures. Reason correctly explains Assertion.
name: Questions Item 16

text: Two resistances \(60.36\,\Omega\) and \(30.09\,\Omega\) are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is
explanation: Parallel resistance \(R_p = \frac{60.36 \times 30.09}{60.36 + 30.09} = 20\,\Omega\). Maximum percentage error calculation yields absolute error \(\pm 0.08\,\Omega\).
name: Questions Item 17

text: In a system, unit of mass is \(A\) kg, length is \(B\) m and time is \(C\) s, then the value of \(10\,\text{N}\) in this system is
name: Questions Item 18

text: Assertion (A) Energy per unit volume and angular momentum can be added dimensionally. Reason (R) Physical quantities having same dimensions can be added or subtracted.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
explanation: Energy per unit volume = \([ML^{-1}T^{-2}]\), angular momentum = \([ML^{2}T^{-1}]\). Dimensions differ, so Assertion (A) is false. Reason (R) is true.
name: Questions Item 19

text: Match the measurements given in List I with the number of significant figures given in List II. (A) 74.083 (B) 0.029 (C) 0.002407 (D) \(2.74 \times 10^{7}\)
explanation: (A) 74.083 → 5 sig figs (IV), (B) 0.029 → 2 sig figs (III), (C) 0.002407 → 4 sig figs (II), (D) \(2.74 \times 10^{7}\) → 3 sig figs (I). Correct matching: IV, III, II, I.
name: Questions Item 20

text: If \(A\) represents Boltzmann constant, \(B\) represents Planck’s constant and \(C\) represents speed of light in vacuum, then the quantity having the dimensions of \(A^{4} B^{-3} C^{-2}\) is
name: Questions Item 21
